problem: Bill can buy jags, jigs and jogs for $\$1$, $\$2$ and $\$7$ each, respectively. What is the largest number of jogs he can purchase if he must buy at least one of each item and will spend exactly $\$50$?

classified_questions.question: What items can Bill purchase and what is the cost of each item?
classified_questions.label: f

classified_questions.question: What is the total amount of money that Bill has available to spend?
classified_questions.label: f

classified_questions.question: If Bill wants to buy at least one of each item, how much money would be spent on jags, jigs, and jogs?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: How many different combinations of jags, jigs, and jogs can Bill purchase to spend exactly $50?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: Can Bill purchase 7 jogs? Why or why not?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: What is the maximum number of jogs that Bill can purchase?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: Can you provide an example of a combination of jags, jigs, and jogs that would allow Bill to spend exactly $50 while buying the largest number of jogs?
classified_questions.label: b
